channel.title: Matthew Brensilver Dharma Talks
channel.description: A merged podcast feed of Matthew Brensilver Dharma talks from AudioDharma and Dharma Seed.
channel.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
channel.itunes:summary: A merged podcast feed of Matthew Brensilver Dharma talks from AudioDharma and Dharma Seed.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Difficult for the Self to Understand what it can't do
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with simple orientation to body, breath, and a warm attitude of love, then settles into a steady practice of resting attention without overworking it. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes the difference between what we can intentionally do and what belongs to awareness itself, repeatedly returning to the question of “what is your job” and what is not. The talk moves toward a quiet humility: attention can be directed, but awareness and the unfolding of Dharma cannot be forced or owned by the self.
Chapters: 00:11 Arrive in the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=11 00:50 A relaxed attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=50 02:04 Hope, fear, and re-dedication - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=124 03:24 What is your job - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=204 07:54 Staying with the step - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=474 15:23 The self and the path - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=923 16:43 Awareness larger than story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=1003 23:13 Instruction and limitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=1393
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25380

item.title: Dharmette: Insight & Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores how insight and love are not separate paths, but mutually reinforcing responses to lived experience. He argues that when impermanence, unsatisfactoriness, and not-self are felt deeply rather than understood abstractly, they soften the heart and make care, non-violence, and forgiveness more natural. The talk moves from a question about wisdom and compassion into a grounded reflection on grief, selfing, and the relief of non-ownership.
Chapters: 00:17 Insight and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=17 02:01 Wisdom from ordinariness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=121 03:27 Gunaratana on insight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=207 04:55 Heartbreak before freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=295 06:23 Dukkha softens the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=383 08:00 Facing impermanence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=480 10:38 Letting self relax - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=638 13:34 Empty center, open heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25381/?t=814
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25381

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness is reverential about all phenomena
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on meeting experience without sorting it into pleasant and unpleasant. The practice moves from settling the body, to softening judgment, to recognizing awareness as naturally respectful of whatever arises. He closes by inviting listeners to trust their own wisdom and to speak to themselves as they would guide another meditator.
Chapters: 00:11 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=11 01:13 Feeling Good, Feeling Bad - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=73 02:56 Releasing Preference - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=176 04:17 Just Breathe - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=257 07:01 Reverence for Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=421 10:04 Steady Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=604 12:24 Effort and Ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=744 14:00 Meeting the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=840 18:59 Listening Within - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=1139
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: Very brief overview of Shinzen Young’s approach
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver gives a concise overview of Shinzen Young’s approach to mindfulness, treating it less as a vague quality than as a workable training map. He breaks the method into three interlocking skills—concentration, sensory clarity, and equanimity—then shows how that framework leads to three aims for practice, three sensory domains, and three basic modes of meditation. The talk is brief, practical, and reflective, with a few comments on why this system has stayed influential in his own teaching.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the sketch - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=0 01:24 What mindfulness means - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=84 02:13 Three skills together - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=133 03:50 The three skills unpacked - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=230 07:36 Why practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=456 10:22 Where to practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=622 12:23 Three practice modes - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=743 15:20 Closing invitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25294/?t=920
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25294

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation on opening to this moment by softening obvious tension and the subtler habits of control. The talk moves from surrender and the release of dividing lines, toward a steadier sense of urgency that is not driven by desperation. It closes with a quiet inquiry into awareness, vulnerability, and what can actually be harmed.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening to this moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=12 01:19 Putting down control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=79 05:39 Breath and bracing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=339 07:51 Urgency without strain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=471 13:50 Awake, not deliberate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=830 17:48 Awareness and vulnerability - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=1068 23:42 What can be harmed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=1422
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: Ajahn Chah's Advice to Woman on Deathbed
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins by naming his own difficulty finding something to say in a time marked by war, anger, and helplessness, then turns to a remembered teaching from Ajahn Chah as a steadier response. The core of the talk is a deathbed instruction on letting go, seeing body and mind as not-self, and taking awareness and the breath as refuge. Matthew closes by reflecting that these words do not answer everything, but they can still matter as a necessary practice and a kind of love without control.
Chapters: 00:11 Searching for a talk - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=11 02:53 Turning to a master - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=173 03:57 Letting body and world go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=237 06:06 Awareness as refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=366 07:57 No self, no attachment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=477 09:52 A gift of Dhamma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=592 11:41 What words cannot do - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25235/?t=701
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25235

item.title: Working with Self-Hatred
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver responds to a question about self-hatred and self-compassion by gently questioning the assumptions underneath both. He suggests looking for the functions self-hate serves, staying with suffering long enough to see it clearly, and trusting that deeper change may come through love, not force. The talk moves from practical inquiry into a broader reflection on faith, conditioning, retreat, and the ways deep feeling can open a different relationship to the self.
Chapters: 00:12 The question of self-hate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=12 01:23 Finding what fits - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=83 02:23 What self-hatred protects - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=143 04:01 Ordinariness and suffering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=241 05:53 Faith through suffering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=353 08:02 No spiritual battle - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=482 10:12 Beyond the self game - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=612 12:15 Retreat, mortality, and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25131/?t=735
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Dedicating our practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on arriving, softening, and dedicating practice to the welfare of self and others. The instruction moves from settling body and mind, to letting go of bracing and defensiveness, to reflecting on love, finitude, and the seriousness of practice without strain. The tone stays steady and unforced, with long stretches of repeated phrases that support breathing and attention.
Chapters: 00:30 Arriving Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=30 02:01 Putting It Down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=121 03:20 Dedicating Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=200 10:29 Love and Finitude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=629 12:16 Quiet Repetition - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=736 19:44 Seeing Differently - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=1184
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: safe to relax
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentle calibration of effort: not too much, not too little, but what this moment asks for. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to breathing in a way that signals safety to the body-mind, so that even amid vulnerability and imperfection it can be okay to relax and let go. Midway through, he brings in a brief Rumi reference to clarify the difference between ordinary rest and the release that comes from stepping out of self-view and possession.
Chapters: 00:12 Calibrating Effort - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=12 01:53 Ease in Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=113 04:34 Safety in the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=274 08:58 Rumi and Rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=538 13:19 Making No Objects - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=799 17:30 Trusting Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=1050
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25072

item.title: A Meaningful Dharma Life
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on a quieter promise of Dharma practice: not only greater happiness, but a life that becomes more meaningful. He traces how the mind keeps making meaning, how practice loosens that habit, and how ethical conduct, vow, and lineage can build a more grounded sense of purpose. The talk moves from a research definition of meaning to personal examples, then toward compassion, non-regret, and the consolation of being part of a long stream of wisdom and love.
Chapters: 00:22 The promise of practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=22 01:18 Meaning and its making - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=78 03:31 What meaning links - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=211 05:04 A personal turn - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=304 05:59 Drama, culture, and emptiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=359 08:58 Meaning from within - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=538 10:29 Practice and purpose - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=629 12:23 Vow and non-regret - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=743 15:04 Lineage and consolation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=904
Source: AudioDharma

He traces how the mind keeps making meaning, how practice loosens that habit, and how ethical conduct, vow, and lineage can build a more grounded sense of purpose. The talk moves from a research definition of meaning to personal examples, then toward compassion, non-regret, and the consolation of being part of a long stream of wisdom and love.
Chapters: 00:22 The promise of practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=22 01:18 Meaning and its making - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=78 03:31 What meaning links - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=211 05:04 A personal turn - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=304 05:59 Drama, culture, and emptiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=359 08:58 Meaning from within - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=538 10:29 Practice and purpose - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=629 12:23 Vow and non-regret - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=743 15:04 Lineage and consolation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25073/?t=904

item.title: Guided Meditation: Refuge
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on refuge, beginning with repeated invitations to settle in Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha, and to let love, gratitude, or reverence soften the heart. The practice gradually widens from the immediate body and breath to a larger sense of being held by the stream of Dharma across time, before ending with a brief reflective closing that points toward the split between subject and object. The tone stays simple and intimate, with long stretches of repetition and spaciousness rather than a lot of discursive explanation.
Chapters: 00:12 Entering Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=12 01:56 Love and Reverence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=116 03:46 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=226 06:10 Return to Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=370 09:57 Dharma Across Time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=597 15:05 Less Identified - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=905 22:22 Closing Insight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=1342
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: Meditation is so many things
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on meditation as something much larger than attention training: a way of resting, digesting pain, cultivating gratitude, and learning to trust experience as it changes. He moves through peace, suffering, goodness, samadhi, letting go, and the question of what it means to arrive in one’s life. The talk stays practical and restrained, while widening the frame from daily practice to mortality and completion.
Chapters: 00:12 Meditation has many facets - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=12 01:57 Deep rest and wakefulness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=117 03:18 Peace, pain, gratitude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=198 05:31 Earth from space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=331 06:50 Knowing ourselves clearly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=410 08:21 Suffering and goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=501 10:26 Samadhi and letting go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=626 12:23 Past, mortality, completion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25029/?t=743

item.title: Guided Meditation: non-referentiality
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that gradually shifts from settling with a subtly pleasant sensation into a broader invitation to release reference points altogether. The practice moves through body-based cues, softening the mind’s tendency to measure and orient, and then toward a sense of unpartitioned space, non-referential awareness, and less bracing against pain or time. Near the end, the language becomes more spacious and poetic, with reminders that this moment is innocent and that the trees, as a final image, seem to arrive where they are going.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling with the pleasant - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=12 01:17 A soft smile - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=77 02:38 Dropping the vantage point - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=158 04:35 Renounce orientation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=275 06:19 Fear and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=379 08:31 Relaxing more deeply - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=511 17:09 Not bracing against pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1029 21:06 The grammar of self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1266 25:49 Trees and arrival - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1549

item.title: Dharmette: Understanding, Forgiveness & Unconsciousness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver responds to a question about remorse after having done serious harm, especially the pain of not being able to make a coherent story about how it happened. He explores the limits of self-understanding, the role of unconscious forces, and how forgiveness can coexist with grief even when full explanation never arrives. The talk moves toward a gentler view of divided motivation, not-self, and the possibility of taking responsibility without disowning the parts of ourselves we do not fully know.
Chapters: 00:12 The Question of Remorse - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=12 02:09 Witnessing Without Explanation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=129 04:04 Seeking Forgiveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=244 05:55 Reasons and Causes - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=355 08:34 The Unconscious at Work - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=514 09:44 Divided Selves - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=584 12:04 Pleasure and Renunciation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=724 13:34 Love and Heartbreak - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24994/?t=814
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Self, Self-Love, Selflessness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores how insight practice moves between simple clarity and complex psychological terrain, using self, self-love, and selflessness as a dialectic rather than a single answer. He argues that stability and repetition are not obstacles to awakening but the conditions that let disorientation, insight, and relinquishment be integrated safely. Along the way he draws on retreat life, neuroscience, and teachers such as Manindraji, Jack Engler, Ajahn Sucitto, and Thomas Metzinger to show why a workable sense of self can support deeper letting go.
Chapters: 00:09 Simplicity and complexity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=9 01:16 The dharma as dialectic - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=76 04:00 Stability and disorientation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=240 08:18 What self means - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=498 13:21 Learning readiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=801 20:47 Brain balance and retreat - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=1247 25:21 A self that can hold - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=1521 30:13 Self-love and ownership - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=1813 37:28 Unbinding and release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25024/?t=2248
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: Question on Social Anxiety
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver responds to a question about social anxiety by beginning with acceptance rather than self-improvement. He moves from non-resistance and feeling the pain directly into a careful look at shame, objectification, and the fear of being seen by others. The talk closes by turning toward anatta, suggesting that the deepest relief may come through loosening the sense of a fixed self rather than defending it.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Safety of Letting Go
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling the body into a deeper kind of listening, where thought is seen less as a problem than as a protective habit. He gradually reframes thinking, emotion, and even control itself as things that can be met with permission, forgiveness, and mindfulness. The talk ends by opening into the safety that can come from relinquishing control and resting with experience as it is.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: How We Incarnate in Different Contexts
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver uses a line from Ajahn Chah’s aspiration verses to explore how we “incarnate” differently from moment to moment and in different roles. He moves from the apparent stability of personality to the subtler ways sankharas, emotion, and social context shape the self we become in each situation. The talk closes with practical questions for noticing what gets amplified, what gets forgotten, and how Dharma is present or distant in different settings.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief, wry set of Melissa Broder quotations that hold dread and humor together, then turns toward a guided meditation on settling and appreciating what is already present. The practice moves from posture and breath to resting with goodness in the body, noticing ordinary life as quietly sufficient, and softening the mind’s habit of organizing around distress. In the closing stretch, he ties enjoyment, letting go, and the Dharma idea of anicca together, suggesting that fear and worry can relax when the mind stops needing to manage everything.
Chapters: 00:19 Comic Prelude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=19 02:21 Finding Posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=141 05:19 Settling in Goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=319 09:26 Resting With Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=566 13:06 Being Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=786 17:17 Enjoyment Deeply - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1037 19:47 Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1187 23:27 Fear and Uncertainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1407
Source: AudioDharma

The practice moves from posture and breath to resting with goodness in the body, noticing ordinary life as quietly sufficient, and softening the mind’s habit of organizing around distress. In the closing stretch, he ties enjoyment, letting go, and the Dharma idea of anicca together, suggesting that fear and worry can relax when the mind stops needing to manage everything.

item.title: Dharmette: Tranquility, View, Truth
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores a simple but demanding question: in what state do we see most clearly? Drawing on the Buddha’s link between truth and freedom, he argues that clear perception depends on bodily balance, calm, and a mind less crowded by fear, arousal, and narrative certainty. The talk moves from the way mood shapes view to the quieter, more reliable seeing that can arise in tranquility, while still recognizing dukkha and the distortions of greed and hatred.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sitting as if already dead
item.description:
This guided meditation uses the image of sitting "as if already dead" to loosen concern, self-protection, and future bargaining, while keeping the mind very awake. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to softening the body, relaxing the heart, and resting in a way that lets love remain when fear and hatred lose their footing. The practice gradually widens into loving-kindness and a simple instruction to slow down, be still, and let the moment do some of the work.

item.title: Dharmette: Orienting to Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on what it means to orient toward goodness when suffering, fear, and political division can make love seem inadequate or naive. He argues that goodness is not a luxury but something the mind and the world both need, and he connects that claim to a recent peace walk, to the Dalai Lama’s emphasis on compassion as necessity, and to Samuel Bagg’s writing on epistemic trust and democracy. The talk moves from a personal and moral case for goodness into a practical account of how love softens identity, supports solidarity, and makes genuine listening possible.

item.title: Guided Practice: Compassion and Equanimity (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided practice and reflection on compassion and equanimity as two qualities that need to be held in conversation, not treated as a fixed formula. He names compassion as love in the face of suffering and equanimity as love in the face of helplessness, then moves into a brief guided settling, a prayer-like aspiration, and a longer contemplation on grief, mortality, and the limits of what love can change. The talk closes by returning to compassion, with an emphasis on resting without collapse and meeting suffering skillfully.
Chapters: 00:00 Middle path, living balance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=0 01:45 Compassion and equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=105 06:04 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=364 12:02 The trembling heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=722 13:54 Love and mortality - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=834 21:03 Return to compassion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=1263

item.title: Guided Meditation: What Does it Take to Land Here? (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief retreat ritual of surrendering phones and settling into shared silence. Matthew Brensilver then leads a practice centered on letting go safely: meeting fear with awareness, trusting the support of the ground, and allowing the breath to signal ease to the body and mind. In the second half, he shifts to walking meditation, emphasizing simplicity, rhythm, and the felt sense of experience over discursive thinking. The talk closes with practical guidance for where to walk at Spirit Rock and how to adapt the practice if walking is difficult.

item.title: Orienting and ruling out the alternatives to love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens by orienting the retreat and making a case for wholehearted participation, including the value—and limits—of online practice. From there he turns to loving-kindness as a way of ruling out hate and apathy, then explores why those alternatives can feel psychologically useful before showing how compassion, rest, and equanimity can make love sustainable. The talk moves into a guided settling period where he invites listeners to sense what kind of love is needed in the moment, and to rest without slipping into detachment.

item.title: Love, Grief and Inconsolability
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on grief, love, and mortality by moving between Buddhist impermanence teachings, a deathbed instruction from Ajahn Chah, and his own questions about fear and control. He considers what Dharma can and cannot heal, especially when suffering remains irreducible or inconsolable. The talk turns toward a quieter freedom: love that is less entangled with outcome, more willing to simply illuminate.

item.title: Guided Metta meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet introduction to metta as a flexible heart practice rather than a feeling to force. He emphasizes that the Dharma does not ask us to pretend, and that loving-kindness may take different forms depending on the moment: compassion, joy, equanimity, forgiveness, gratitude, or simply a simpler phrase that can be held with sincerity. The talk moves from this framing into guided practice, then into questions about bodily constriction, deservingness, grief, and how love and sorrow are intertwined.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=0 02:07 Finding the right love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=127 05:57 Measureless awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=357 12:00 Guided metta practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=720 40:31 Practice in the world - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=2431 50:20 Constriction and worthiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3020 54:42 Love and grief - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3282 01:00:54 Closing instructions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3654
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Functions of heart practice: cultivation, concentration, purification
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a talk and guided practice on metta as something flexible rather than fixed: a way of meeting the truth of the moment with whatever form of care is actually available. He frames heart practice through three functions—cultivation, concentration, and purification—and emphasizes that the work may look like resting in love, focusing on a phrase, or allowing buried pain to surface. The guided section then moves from settling the body into a slower, patient practice of letting time, words, and goodwill do their work.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=0 02:57 Three functions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=177 06:21 Cultivation and focus - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=381 11:06 Open space of self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=666 12:00 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=720 24:16 Words and reverberation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=1456 31:35 Purification and patience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=1895 33:46 Goodwill and Sangha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=2026
Source: AudioDharma

Matthew Brensilver offers a talk and guided practice on metta as something flexible rather than fixed: a way of meeting the truth of the moment with whatever form of care is actually available. He frames heart practice through three functions—cultivation, concentration, and purification—and emphasizes that the work may look like resting in love, focusing on a phrase, or allowing buried pain to surface. The guided section then moves from settling the body into a slower, patient practice of letting time, words, and goodwill do their work.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=0 02:57 Three functions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=177 06:21 Cultivation and focus - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=381 11:06 Open space of self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=666 12:00 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=720 24:16 Words and reverberation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=1456 31:35 Purification and patience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=1895 33:46 Goodwill and Sangha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24615/?t=2026
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/24615

item.title: Guided Meditation: entering the dharma realm
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that frames practice as intentionally “entering a Dharma realm,” a field of mind shaped by goodness, kindness, clarity, and letting go. He moves through refuge, the subtle reorientation of perception, and the way this ground helps us meet suffering without adding more to it. The talk closes by turning toward love, vow, and service as expressions of a radical non-violent path.
Chapters: 00:11 Taking Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=11 01:07 Why Refuge Matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=67 03:52 Entering the Dharma Field - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=232 07:08 Second Arrow - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=428 08:52 Goodwill and Renunciation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=532 11:24 Subtle Change - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=684 17:11 Breath and Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=1031 22:58 What Love Asks - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24554/?t=1378
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/24554

item.title: Anatta as a cause and effect of freedom
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the relationship between not-self and freedom by asking a practical question: does insight into anatta cause liberation, or does it emerge as a result of the path? He uses the idea of causality to distinguish correlation from cause, then moves through how selfing, shame, suffering, ethics, love, humility, and psychological healing gradually loosen identification. The talk ends by cautioning against treating isolated peak experiences as the whole path, emphasizing a holistic practice that can make freedom and not-self mutually shape one another.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening the question - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=12 01:17 Causality and conditions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=77 03:55 Anatta and freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=235 07:09 De-centering self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=429 09:50 Ethics and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=590 11:20 Humility on the path - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=680 13:31 Healing and fluidity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=811 15:24 Holistic conclusion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24555/?t=924
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Spiritual vs Psychological Health
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the relationship between psychological health and spiritual awakening by laying out four possible ways to understand their connection: separation, continuity, sequence, and mutual threading. He then argues for a more nuanced view, where Dharma both heals psychological pain and reveals deeper layers of conditioning that cannot be reduced to therapy alone. The talk moves from theory into practice-level questions about self-love, disidentification, and why spiritual work often brings old wounds into sharper relief.
Chapters: 00:11 Framing the question - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=11 01:18 Four possible views - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=78 04:46 Language and the absolute - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=286 07:01 Dharma as healing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=421 11:13 Where psychology ends - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=673 14:59 Preconditions for practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=899 18:36 Threaded together - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=1116 25:11 Self-love and not-self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=1511 30:20 Working with deep knots - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24512/?t=1820
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Wise Intention
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores wise intention as something more gradual and relational than a single decision. After a long guided sit, he turns to the gap between intention and behavior, the role of habit, and why sincere practice is less about self-improvement than about letting go of grasping. The talk ends with a wide-ranging dialogue on surrender, addiction, sincerity, and how renunciation, metta, and harmlessness shape daily life.

item.title: TS Eliot and the Mystical Dimension of Buddhism
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the mystical dimension of Buddhism without romanticizing mystical experience. Using William James, T.S. Eliot, Joseph Goldstein, and a short canonical citation, he traces a path of ignorance, dispossession, non-ownership, and relinquishing views. The talk stays close to practice: ordinary life, egoic footing, and the quiet relief that can come from not knowing.
Chapters: 00:11 William James and consciousness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=11 01:56 Mysticism without fetish - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=116 03:21 Renunciation and ego - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=201 05:39 Eliot’s way of ignorance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=339 07:22 The dry terrain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=442 09:05 Keeping balance in not-knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=545 12:20 The practice of don't know - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=740 14:09 Non-attachment and views - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=849 15:53 Refrain and close - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24449/?t=953
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation on relaxing the body and loosening control so experience can be met more simply. The talk moves from basic instructions to a reflection on equanimity, non-proliferation, and how unpleasant experience can be allowed to end without turning into a story. It closes with a gentler, more embodied image of the moment as something that can soften the body-mind and strengthen the case for love.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=12 01:00 Putting down identity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=60 02:02 Letting experience be - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=122 07:10 Equanimity and non-proliferation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=430 09:37 Very alive to this moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=577 11:03 Softening into love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=663 12:39 Closing bell meditation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24450/?t=759
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound and Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on listening as a way of meeting sound, thought, and silence without grasping. The practice moves from simple instruction into a wider field of hearing, including external sounds, internal sounds, equanimity, and a gentle bowing to fear and preoccupation. Near the end, the attention turns toward very distant sounds and the edge where hearing and silence are no longer easy to separate.
Chapters: 01:00 Entering Listening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=60 02:30 Hunger and Sound - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=150 04:00 Listening in All Directions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=240 06:15 Self With No Job - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=375 07:30 No Inside or Outside - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=450 12:20 Equanimity and Nonclinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=740 15:37 Bowing to Preoccupations - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=937 22:33 Edge of Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24399/?t=1353

item.title: Dharmette: Very Condensed Overview of the Path
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a very condensed map of how major Dharma teachings fit together, moving from the problem of seeking peace through possession to the diagnosis of dukkha, anicca, and anatta. He then sketches how practice supports letting go through renunciation, mettā, and harmlessness, and how wise view and ethical discipline stabilize the path. The talk closes by showing how mindfulness and equanimity de-reify experience without becoming cold or abstract, ending with a note that this kind of dissolving is inseparable from love.
Chapters: 00:11 Why a map matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=11 01:32 Naming the real problem - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=92 03:04 Diagnosis as medicine - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=184 04:33 Craving and suffering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=273 06:03 The raft of practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=363 08:11 Wise view and memory - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=491 10:18 Holding forms lightly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=618 11:37 De-reifying experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=697 13:09 Love at the end - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24400/?t=789
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Spiritual Friendship (Berkeley Satellite Daylong)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on spiritual friendship as something that both supports and grows out of practice, beginning with the mind’s social permeability and the need for good influences. He moves through belonging, early dependence, isolation, truthfulness, and the relief of being known without performance. Near the end, he turns to play, creativity, and “feeling real” as ways friendship softens self-consciousness and helps us return to ordinary goodness.
Chapters: 00:09 The mind is permeable - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=9 01:52 Friendship as support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=112 03:58 Belonging and dependency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=238 07:05 Isolation and remoteness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=425 10:39 What spiritual friendship is - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=639 13:55 Truthfulness and sanity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=835 16:02 Being known clearly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=962 40:13 Play and creativity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=2413 43:59 Feeling real - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=2639

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on spiritual friendship as something that both supports and grows out of practice, beginning with the mind’s social permeability and the need for good influences. He moves through belonging, early dependence, isolation, truthfulness, and the relief of being known without performance. Near the end, he turns to play, creativity, and “feeling real” as ways friendship softens self-consciousness and helps us return to ordinary goodness.
Chapters: 00:09 The mind is permeable - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=9 01:52 Friendship as support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=112 03:58 Belonging and dependency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=238 07:05 Isolation and remoteness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=425 10:39 What spiritual friendship is - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=639 13:55 Truthfulness and sanity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=835 16:02 Being known clearly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=962 40:13 Play and creativity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=2413 43:59 Feeling real - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24379/?t=2639

item.title: Guided Meditation: Ease Makes Letting Go Safer
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentler relationship with agitation by finding a breath that carries ease into the body. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes that awareness does not solve every problem, but it can change how problems are carried, making letting go feel safer when there is some stability and warmth present. The practice moves gradually from settling the body to resting in a subtle sense of care, simplicity, and enoughness.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling the breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=12 01:11 Rest with ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=71 02:33 Care for the present - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=153 04:50 Awareness changes carrying - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=290 08:42 Wide world, simple attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=522 14:22 Letting go feels safer - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=862 20:29 Warmth and abiding - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24241/?t=1229
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: Rivers are just rivers
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver uses Dogen’s image of mountains and rivers to trace a practice arc from ordinary certainty, through destabilizing insight, to a more mature ordinariness. He moves from the mind’s assumptions and a brief neuroscience aside into meditation as a kind of reversal of our usual attentional habits, where the self feels less fixed and experience more fluid. The talk ends by softening the apparent paradox: awakening does not replace ordinary life so much as make love and intimacy more visible within it.
Chapters: 00:52 Opening the Theme - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=52 02:05 Ordinary Certainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=125 03:56 Looking Inward - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=236 05:32 Meditation Reverses Habit - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=332 07:36 Fluid Perception - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=456 09:28 The Weird Phase - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=568 12:07 New Baselines - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=727 14:35 Love Remains - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24242/?t=875
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: The What and How of Investigation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores investigation as a practice of attention rather than a search for quick answers. He questions the urge to turn practice into an algorithm, distinguishes genuine inquiry from compulsive thinking, and shows how insight often appears when the mind relaxes its demand to know. The talk closes by framing dhammavichaya as a way of looking that avoids premature story-making and stays open to what is not yet clear.
Chapters: 00:16 The Question of Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=16 01:25 Letting the Question Dissolve - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=85 02:25 Answers and Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=145 04:56 Sticky Thought - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=296 06:50 Attention, Not Answers - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=410 08:41 Relaxing the Need to Know - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=521 10:57 Dhammavichaya - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=657 13:27 Path and Goal - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24176/?t=807
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Activity and Receptivity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on finding a workable balance between activity and receptivity. Using the image of entering water, he explores how the body gives consent to letting go, how faith and safety shape surrender, and how attention can be directed without becoming rigid. The practice widens into noticing when effort is skillful and when receptivity becomes the steadier ground.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Bright, Steady, Open
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on bright, steady, open awareness: knowing experience without collapsing into the self. He emphasizes practicing with clarity, stability, equanimity, and a gentle relationship to conditioning, then widens the frame toward rest, humility, and spaciousness. The meditation closes by returning to the simple fact of being neither everything nor nothing, but present and responsive.

item.title: Dharmette: Empathic Resonance
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores empathy as something that begins with close attention to one’s own mind and then extends outward without collapsing self and other. He distinguishes emotional resonance from perspective-taking, emphasizes humility and nonprojective listening, and returns several times to the need for the “as-if” condition in compassionate contact. The talk moves from mindfulness as self-directed empathy to a more careful account of calibrated, boundaried presence in healing relationships.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax and release
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on relaxing body and mind, with attention to the ways future-thinking, control, and spiritual striving can tighten experience. The practice moves toward letting go more deliberately, including a gentle but sometimes firmer sense of renunciation, and then into refuge and a simple resting with meaning-making set aside. The talk stays close to the felt experience of this moment, returning again and again to stability, clarity, and enoughness.

item.title: Dharmette: attention, awareness, mindfulness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver distinguishes attention from awareness and mindfulness, arguing that practice is not just about focusing on an object but also noticing the background quality of the mind that knows it. He moves from a simple model of spotlighted attention to a broader account of receptive awareness, relaxation, and the way craving can quietly shape what seems like curiosity. The talk ends by reframing mindfulness as a subtle freedom: seeing clinging as an object rather than obeying it.

item.title: Living Alongside Questions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores what it means to live with impermanence, uncertainty, and unfinished questions rather than trying to force life into certainty. He moves from the mind’s habit of assuming continuity to the way fixed views can harden into fear and harm, then into a gentler practice of opening to love, understanding, equanimity, and the limits of control. The talk closes by looking honestly at recurring patterns, suffering that returns over decades, and the possibility of staying open anyway.
Chapters: 00:01 Settling into the room - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=1 01:34 The illusion of permanence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=94 05:15 Impermanence and uncertainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=315 07:30 Views and certainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=450 10:11 A life with questions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=611 12:29 Two koans of practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=749 16:45 Love as understanding - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=1005 22:45 The circle of concern - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=1365 27:22 Compassion and equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93338/?t=1642

He moves from the mind’s habit of assuming continuity to the way fixed views can harden into fear and harm, then into a gentler practice of opening to love, understanding, equanimity, and the limits of control. The talk closes by looking honestly at recurring patterns, suffering that returns over decades, and the possibility of staying open anyway.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Allowing (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a very simple release: forgive all phenomena, including the movements of liking, disliking, and selfing. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to not fighting inner conflict, then gradually opens the practice into softening, trusting awareness, and letting the present moment be exactly as it is. In the closing period he offers a strong permission practice—nothing done in these minutes is wrong—before ending with brief retreat announcements.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening forgiveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=0 01:00 Not fighting resistance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=60 10:18 Softening into Dharma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=618 17:26 Settling the spasms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1046 21:46 No autobiography needed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1306 26:12 Yielding to awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1572 31:20 Nothing is wrong - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1880 39:14 Closing announcements - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=2354
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax a Little Further (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided retreat meditation built around one repeated instruction: relax a little further. He links that softening to letting go of assumptions about self, progress, and control, and to recognizing practice as a balance between effort and receptivity. As the meditation deepens, he turns toward attention, pain, and refuge, ending with a quiet reminder that simply being alive is enough.

item.title: Reflections on Time in Dharma: Surrendering to Time, Healing Time, Not-Making Time (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on time as a central pressure in practice: too much time, too little time, and the urge to fill it. From there he moves through boredom, grief, retreat structure, samadhi, impermanence, memory, and self-view, gradually shifting time from something that burdens the heart to something that can soften it. The talk closes by suggesting that when the churning of becoming quiets, there is a kind of refuge in timelessness and simple presence.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening: time as the problem - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=0 02:51 Busyness and boredom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=171 08:36 Grief widens the view - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=516 10:49 Retreat and the schedule - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=649 15:17 Let time work - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=917 22:38 Impermanence in real time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=1358 26:14 Presence and the past - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=1574 33:16 Ghosts into ancestors - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=1996 38:21 Beyond self and time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93302/?t=2301
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing all clinging
item.description:
This guided meditation explores relaxation as a form of surrender rather than mere physical softening. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to letting go of control, stepping out of narrow identifications, and allowing the mind to rest without feeding familiar grooves of worry and selfing. The practice moves toward tenderness, forgiveness, and trust in the goodness already present, ending in a quieter sense of awareness less dominated by self.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling into surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=0 00:50 Releasing the familiar - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=50 04:37 Fatigue with old patterns - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=277 09:06 Longing to rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=546 10:58 Stepping out of identification - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=658 14:40 Forgiveness and kindness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=880 17:22 Trusting goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=1042 22:10 Quiet ending - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24004/?t=1330
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: PR Campaign for Non-Hatred
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on what part of the Dharma could have public reach in a time shaped by anger and polarization. He argues that non-hatred may be the most realistic and useful teaching to broadcast, distinguishing it from love while grounding it in bodily, relational, and canonical evidence. The talk closes with a William Stafford poem that deepens the call for mutual recognition and clear signals in dark times.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: How distant is freedom?
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by turning attention toward a felt sense of freedom and asking how distance itself is created by the mind. Matthew Brensilver invites a gentle, less controlling relationship to breath and body, then widens into lineage, trust, and the recognition that mindfulness is not another project of self-management. The talk settles into a quiet instruction to rest in the present moment without keeping constant tabs on what is right or wrong.
Chapters: 00:12 How Far Is Peace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=12 01:00 All Practice Is Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=60 03:54 Taking Our Cues - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=234 05:54 Softening Control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=354 08:36 Boundaries Without Grasping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=516 11:24 Mindfulness and Ungovernability - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=684 13:07 Trusting the Moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=787 22:09 Resting in Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23939/?t=1329
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharmette: 'Protect Empathy at All Costs and Live Groovy Lives'
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a reflection on Jim Jarmusch and a remark about art changing the world by opening people to one another. From there he develops a quiet argument that Dharma, sangha, and practice can be understood as forms of cultivating beauty and protecting empathy, both inwardly and socially. A personal story about Joe Strummer becomes the talk’s central refrain: protect empathy at all costs and live groovy lives.
Chapters: 00:12 Art Opens the Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=12 01:46 Strummer's Charge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=106 03:08 Dharma as Art - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=188 04:17 Beauty and Sangha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=257 05:40 Abiding in What We Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=340 07:31 Expanding the Circle - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=451 09:40 Seeing Others Clearly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=580 11:44 A Lineage of Care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23938/?t=704
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Dharma Practice and the Cultivation of Power
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins by acknowledging the usual Dharma emphasis on softening, vulnerability, surrender, and the limits of what our love can reach. He then pivots to a more unusual framing: that the path also cultivates power — the strength to face truth, tolerate pain, understand causality, and become less dependent on conditions. The talk closes by contrasting this inner power with fear, delusion, and self-view, and by suggesting that Dharma can offer a steadier happiness that is less fragile and more trustworthy.
Chapters: 00:12 Softening and helplessness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=12 02:55 A different kind of power - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=175 05:00 Truth requires strength - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=300 06:56 Causality and relinquishment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=416 09:01 Independent in the Dharma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=541 10:27 Refusing cultural confusion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=627 12:23 Power and trust - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23888/?t=743
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23888

item.title: Guided Meditation: Internal and External Sound and Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on listening: to external sound, internal sound, and the silence around and within them. The practice moves gently from noticing the field of hearing to softening identification with thinking, then toward receptivity, non-preference, and a quieter sense of presence. He also draws on a Shakespeare line, a brief Mother Teresa story, and Martin Luther King Jr. to underline how listening can be both contemplative and ethical.
Chapters: 00:00 Entering Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=0 01:24 Outer and Inner Sound - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=84 03:28 No Preference - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=208 07:31 Receptive Listening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=451 12:09 Body and Space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=729 15:10 Meaning and Fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=910 16:57 Dharma as Blessing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=1017 19:32 Listening in Prayer - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=1172 27:04 Nonviolence in Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93052/?t=1624
Source: Dharma Seed

The practice moves gently from noticing the field of hearing to softening identification with thinking, then toward receptivity, non-preference, and a quieter sense of presence. He also draws on a Shakespeare line, a brief Mother Teresa story, and Martin Luther King Jr. to underline how listening can be both contemplative and ethical.

item.title: The Circuitous Journey to Presence through Past and Future
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores why “be present” is not a simple instruction but the fruit of a long process of seeing through memory, anticipation, and the habits that organize selfhood. He moves from humor and personal recollection into a careful argument about pain, grief, and the way mindfulness makes the past less personal and the future less compelling. The talk closes by returning to time, fear, and freedom, with a poetic coda from Bob Dylan.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Question - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=0 03:43 Presence and the Past - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=223 09:59 Change and Training - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=599 13:38 Past as Refuge and Burden - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=818 20:51 Listening to Sorrow - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=1251 26:30 Making Memory Legible - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=1590 32:19 The Urge to Fill Time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=1939 39:22 Futurelessness and Freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93051/?t=2362
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Forgiving Body, Mind and World
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation built around forgiveness as a practical loosening of friction rather than a forced state of peace. The practice moves from sensing what the mind needs, through body-based softening and truce with pain, toward a spacious awareness that can hold reactions, fear, hope, and ordinary sounds without struggle. It ends with a quiet inquiry into what awareness remains even when control drops away.

item.title: Active and Receptive Dimensions of Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames practice as a balance between doing and receiving, using the Middle Path to explore how attention, patience, and renunciation each need their counterpart. He draws on William James, Ajahn Sucitto, Thich Nhat Hanh, Adler, and Mahasi-style effort language to show both the usefulness and the limits of willpower in Dharma practice. The talk gradually moves toward a quieter trust: that healing depends on conditions, that time can become an ally, and that practice often works by softening rather than forcing.
Chapters: 00:49 Middle Path Framing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=49 04:01 Activity and Receptivity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=241 07:39 Letting Go Gradually - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=459 09:55 Training Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=595 15:34 Patience With the Path - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=934 21:45 Renunciation and Enough - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=1305 27:14 What We Cannot Do - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=1634 30:30 Limits of Willfulness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=1830 39:54 Receiving the Blessings - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93049/?t=2394

item.title: Guided Meditation: Urgency of Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on the "urgency" that can arise from feeling. The practice begins with settling the body and breathing, then turns toward noticing hidden agendas, control strategies, and the way emotional surges can seem like commands. As the meditation deepens, he repeatedly returns to relaxing, absorbing feeling, and letting breathing become safe again.

item.title: Attention Models the Object, Mindfulness Models the Attention
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a few words about listening to Dharma talks and the mood of retreat, then turns to the first-day forces that often dominate practice: hindrances, discomfort, and the effort to stay simple with unpleasant experience. From there he builds a careful distinction between attention and mindfulness, using the Satipatthana Sutta, the Dhammapada, and a modern philosopher’s account of mind-wandering to show how perception, identification, and self-modeling shift from moment to moment. The talk closes with a practical emphasis on allowing experience without trying to improve it, and on recognizing that real mindfulness includes awareness of how attention itself is functioning.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and retreat frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=0 01:51 The first-day hindrances - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=111 05:59 Mindfulness in the sutta - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=359 08:03 Mind-made experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=483 12:28 Mind-wandering and self-models - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=748 24:08 What attention does - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=1448 29:40 Control versus connection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=1780 34:37 Allowing instead of improving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93047/?t=2077
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing and Letting Feeling be Feeling
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a simple, careful relationship to pleasant and unpleasant feeling: know it as it is, and notice what softens when it no longer has to mean something about you. The instruction moves from relaxing obvious tension into a narrower and then wider field of attention, including breath, body, and difficult experience with a light touch of kindness. It ends with a quiet emphasis on not bracing against experience and on the felt necessity of love.

item.title: Guided Meditation: moving towards compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how compassion can grow from directly meeting suffering rather than turning away from it. He begins with an Issa poem about dukkha and then repeatedly returns to simple supports: settling the body, soothing the heart, and allowing mindfulness to include sorrow and happiness. The practice gradually widens into a steadier wish for less suffering, with imagery, silent phrases, and a sense of protecting love as it meets pain.

item.title: Dharmette: Opportunities and Complexities of Speaking about Awakening
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on why teachers may or may not speak explicitly about awakening, using personal anecdotes and several Dharma and psychology references to show both the value and the risks of doing so. He moves from the Buddha’s direct language about liberation, to the different needs of group and one-on-one teaching, and then to the tension between urgency, patience, and ordinary psychological healing. The talk ends with a careful reminder that awakening is hard to describe without turning it into another project of the self.
Chapters: 00:18 A Pep Talk Gone Wrong - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=18 01:13 Freedom Beyond Conditions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=73 02:49 The Buddha's Straight Talk - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=169 04:05 Group Context Matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=245 05:20 Psychological Shifts - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=320 07:01 Urgency and Patience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=421 08:44 Many Fruits of Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=524 10:39 The Self's Difficulties - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=639 13:12 Complicated Cause and Effect - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23819/?t=792
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: don't wait to let go
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with posture, settling, and a simple bodily yielding to gravity and breath. Matthew then turns toward impermanence, sufficiency, and the instruction not to delay letting go, before opening into a brief reflection on loving-kindness and the widening that can come when there is more space around grief. The final minutes include a personal remembrance of Kathleen Haviland, which grounds the talk in loss, relationship, and the practical compassion of practice.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=0 02:41 Yielding to gravity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=161 06:23 Finitude and enough - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=383 12:16 Do not wait - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=736 13:08 Resting after surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=788 22:25 Practice loving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1345 23:44 More space around pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1424 25:59 Remembering Kathleen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1559
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23721
item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
This guided meditation begins with posture, settling, and a simple bodily yielding to gravity and breath. Matthew then turns toward impermanence, sufficiency, and the instruction not to delay letting go, before opening into a brief reflection on loving-kindness and the widening that can come when there is more space around grief. The final minutes include a personal remembrance of Kathleen Haviland, which grounds the talk in loss, relationship, and the practical compassion of practice.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=0 02:41 Yielding to gravity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=161 06:23 Finitude and enough - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=383 12:16 Do not wait - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=736 13:08 Resting after surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=788 22:25 Practice loving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1345 23:44 More space around pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1424 25:59 Remembering Kathleen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1559

item.title: Dharmette: Uprooting vs Allowing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver answers a question about when to allow experience and when to actively work with unwholesome mind states. He emphasizes that practice is not a fixed hierarchy of techniques, but a matter of discernment, training, and what actually reduces suffering. The talk moves from restraint and equanimity to more active cognitive methods, then into listening for the deeper need beneath jealousy, anger, fear, and self-protection.
Chapters: 00:12 The question of technique - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=12 01:43 Don't fetishize technique - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=103 02:30 Training before crisis - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=150 04:23 Equanimity and aversion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=263 05:58 Marinating versus being with - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=358 07:49 Active redirection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=469 10:12 Listen for the signal - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=612 12:36 Self, fear, and peace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23722/?t=756
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23722

item.title: Dharmette: NIsargadatta, Nothing, Everything, Wisdom, Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a Nisargadatta quote and uses it to frame meditation as a lived tension between emptiness and boundless love. He then moves through how practice can thin out selfing, feeling tone, and the sense of an observer, before turning to the tenderness that appears when self-clinging loosens. The talk closes with a Shinzen Young passage that gathers the talk into a patient, alternating recognition of nothingness and reified experience.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening quote - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=12 01:26 Two poles of practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=86 02:38 Precise experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=158 04:11 Phenomena dissolving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=251 06:21 Self becomes space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=381 08:03 Desert of the real - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=483 09:59 Love after emptiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=599 12:38 Seeking settles - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=758 13:38 Shinzen's summary - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23668/?t=818
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23668

item.title: Guided Metta Meditation - Metta for All Beings (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames metta as one of the Brahma-viharas, or “four species of love,” then shows how loving-kindness can function as cultivation, concentration, and purification. The talk moves from the way metta loosens self-view to a guided practice that begins with the body and phrases, then opens outward to all beings. It ends by holding the sincerity of the wish alongside the fact that suffering is vast and unresolved.
Chapters: 00:00 Four kinds of love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=0 01:57 Love changes self-view - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=117 05:08 Measureless and quiet - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=308 06:23 Cultivation and purification - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=383 11:48 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=708 22:44 Phrases in the present - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1364 26:46 Resting in kindness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1606 31:13 Love in hard places - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1873 33:15 All beings at ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1995
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/90257/
